verdict: YES -- per-step confidence carries WHERE the model slipped, not just THAT it did, and it SURVIVES de-trending (rules out 'confidence just decays with position'). Sanity: per-step error 0.31, single-slip 0.38 of error-chains, P non-degenerate (mean 0.835 sd 0.211). Per-step error prediction: pooled AUROC 0.732, DE-TRENDED (position-controlled) 0.754. THE DIP IS AT THE ORIGIN: mean de-trended confidence by offset-from-first-error is minimized at offset 0 (-0.1459), high just before (+0.2273 at -2), recovering after. LOCALIZATION on SINGLE-slip chains (n=137, well-posed): de-trended-residual argmin hits the slip 0.562 (raw-conf 0.635) vs position-prior 0.358 vs uniform 0.186. REPAIR (oracle-downstream, redo from the located step): fixes 0.562 of single-slip chains at avg 3.78 steps vs redo-all 5.63. CAVEAT: multi-slip chains (224) -- argmin finds AN error 0.759 but the FIRST only 0.272 (several low-confidence steps). Deployable targeted repair, strongest when the model slips once.
